primary_concern: Sepsis with acute kidney injury (AKI) and coagulopathy
clinical_summary: Elevated lactate (4.2 mmol/L) and leukocytosis (14.2 K/uL) meet Sepsis-3 criteria for sepsis risk. Platelets are persistently low (<100 K/uL) with SOFA coagulation scoring threshold crossed. AKI Stage 3 (creatinine 8.9 mg/dL) is present despite exclusion of the creatinine outlier, suggesting progressive renal failure. Bilirubin elevation (1.4 mg/dL) and anemia (Hb 9.8 g/dL) indicate possible hepatic involvement or hemolysis.

prioritized_risks.explanation: Lactate >2 mmol/L, WBC >12 K/uL, and elevated creatinine meet Sepsis-3 criteria for suspected infection plus SOFA score increase ≥2
Creatinine elevation to Stage 3 (8.9 mg/dL) meets KDIGO criteria for AKI
prioritized_risks.threshold: Creatinine rise ≥0.3 mg/dL in 48h or ≥1.5x baseline in 7d

prioritized_risks.explanation: Platelets <100 K/uL meets SOFA coagulation scoring threshold
Lactate >2 mmol/L present but vasopressor requirements unknown
prioritized_risks.threshold: Vasopressor-dependent hypotension + lactate >2 mmol/L after fluids

recommended_actions: Initiate broad-spectrum antibiotics per sepsis protocol
Obtain blood cultures and infection source control assessment
Start renal replacement therapy for AKI Stage 3
Monitor platelet transfusion threshold (<20 K/uL) and coagulation factors
Reassess fluid status and consider vasopressor support if hypotensive
doctor_handoff: Review cultures/source control for sepsis, confirm vasopressor requirements for septic shock criteria, and evaluate for AKI reversal strategies. Monitor platelet trends for transfusion needs.

disease_progression.observation: Initial sepsis markers: WBC 14.2 K/uL, Hb 9.8 g/dL, Bilirubin 1.4 mg/dL
AKI Stage 1 (Creatinine 2.1 mg/dL) with platelet decline to 95 K/uL
Progression to AKI Stage 3 (Creatinine 8.9 mg/dL) despite outlier exclusion

excluded_outliers.reason: Rapid change detected — Creatinine changed by 6.5 mg/dL within 6.0h (max allowed: 2.0). Probable measurement error or specimen issue. Section 15.2 — rise > 2.0 mg/dL in < 6h.
